title: Authorized fetch enforcement for actor document GETs
description:
## What
When the server config has authorized_fetch=true, require a valid HTTP signature on all GET requests to /users/:username and /users/:username/* (actor document, outbox, followers, following). Reject unsigned requests with 401 and a WWW-Authenticate header. This prevents anonymous scraping of follower graphs.

## Why
Authorized fetch is a privacy feature adopted by mastodon.social and GoToSocial. Without it, anyone can enumerate followers/following without authentication. Part of sub-epic 8-B federation lifecycle.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: test mastodon.social authorized fetch behavior by sending unsigned GET to a locked account's actor URI and documenting the exact 401 response including WWW-Authenticate header format; read activitypub-federation crate docs for HTTP signature verification on incoming GETs; document the exact Signature header format expected.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add authorized_fetch boolean to server config (default false); add Tower middleware or axum extractor that, when authorized_fetch=true, parses the Signature header from GET requests to /users/* routes; verify the signature using the actor's public key (fetch actor if not cached); reject with 401 WWW-Authenticate: Signature realm=... if invalid or missing; cache actor public keys in memory (LRU, 100 entries, 5min TTL) to avoid refetching on every GET.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests with authorized_fetch=true in config; send unsigned GET to /users/testuser -> assert 401; send GET with valid test keypair signature -> assert 200 actor document returned; verify WWW-Authenticate header format matches mastodon.social fixture.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social live 401 response fixture (curl a locked account's actor URI without Signature header); HTTP Signatures draft-cavage-http-signatures spec for WWW-Authenticate format.
- Test approach: integration test with in-memory SQLite and test RSA keypair (generated fresh, not the code-under-test keypair); happy path (valid signature -> 200); edge cases: no Signature header -> 401, malformed Signature header -> 401, expired Date header (>12h old) -> 401, authorized_fetch=false -> 200 with no signature.

## Defensive programming checklist
- Never reject based on Content-Type alone — always verify the actual cryptographic signature
- Cache actor public keys with TTL, not indefinitely (key rotation must take effect)
- Reject if Date header is more than 12 hours old (replay attack prevention)
- Log rejected requests at INFO level: requester IP, missing/invalid signature reason
- No .unwrap() in middleware path
- authorized_fetch=false (default) must not add any overhead to GET requests
